mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that supports corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, or housing starts and retail sales diverge without affecting equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average reflects weakening manufacturing output that triggers short-term risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive during industrial production contractions, or the signal loses predictive power when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate combined with stable or declining inflation indicates real rate compression that boosts equity risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term produces a negative coefficient in out-of-sample regression, or SPX declines when real rates fall below 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly declines in total nonfarm payrolls directly reduce aggregate wage income and consumer spending capacity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no negative correlation with payroll declines over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5% annualized confirms robust economic expansion that lifts corporate profit forecasts, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 70% of triggered instances, or the threshold fails to separate winning and losing periods with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling industrial production and rising unemployment signals a supply-side recessionary shock that compresses equity valuations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields positive forward returns, or the mechanism fails to predict direction when inflation remains above 4%.
